evals.name: Play audio file through specific device
evals.description: User wants to play a WAV file through a named output device with sequential before/after comparison
evals.prompt: I have two WAV files: 'before.wav' and 'after.wav' (both 48 kHz stereo). Write MATLAB code to play them back-to-back through my 'Speakers (RME UFX+ USB 3.0)' device so I can compare them. I want a 1-second gap between them.

context.setup: Audio Toolbox is available. Device 'Speakers (RME UFX+ USB 3.0)' is present.

assertions.description: Uses audiostreamer, not sound/audioplayer
Specifies player mode or uses play method
Does not use sound() or soundsc() for sequential playback
Calls release(as) or stop(as) for cleanup at end
assertions.expected: Code includes explicit teardown with release() or stop() followed by release()

assertions.description: Specifies the device by name via Player property
assertions.expected: Player property is set to the RME device name, either at construction or via property assignment

assertions.description: Waits for playback to complete before releasing

evals.name: Measure impulse response via ASIO loopback
evals.description: User wants to measure room/device impulse response using sweep excitation on ASIO hardware
evals.prompt: I need to measure the impulse response of my audio system. I have an RME MADIface USB with ASIO driver and a loopback cable on channels 1 (out) and 1 (in). Write MATLAB code to generate a swept-sine excitation, play it out channel 1, record from channel 1, and compute the impulse response. Use 48 kHz sample rate.

context.setup: Audio Toolbox and DSP System Toolbox available. ASIO MADIface USB device present with loopback on channel 1.

assertions.description: Uses full-duplex mode for simultaneous play/record
Uses sweeptone for excitation signal
Uses impzest to deconvolve the IR
Specifies ASIO driver
Uses playrec for synchronized play+record with blocking output
assertions.expected: Uses playrec(as, x, ...) with an output argument to get synchronized recording, OR uses playrec followed by waitfor and read

evals.name: Callback-driven continuous level meter
evals.description: User wants a real-time level meter using recorder callbacks
evals.prompt: Write MATLAB code for a continuous audio level meter. I want to record from my default input device at 44100 Hz and print the RMS level every 1024 samples using a callback. The meter should run until I press Ctrl+C. Make sure it handles cleanup properly.

context.setup: Audio Toolbox available. Default recording device present.

assertions.description: Uses recorder mode
Sets RecorderFcn callback
Sets RecorderMinSamples to 1024
Callback accepts exactly 2 arguments (obj, event or ~)
assertions.expected: RecorderFcn callback signature has exactly 2 input arguments (e.g., @(obj, ~) or @(obj, event))

assertions.description: Uses try-catch or onCleanup for robust teardown
assertions.expected: Code handles Ctrl+C gracefully via try-catch around the blocking wait, or onCleanup, ensuring stop() and release() are called

assertions.description: Starts recording with record(as) or start(as)
assertions.expected: After setting RecorderFcn, calls record(as) or start(as) to begin the callback-driven recording

evals.name: Measure impulse response with tone sweep
evals.description: User wants to measure a system impulse response using their sound card and a tone sweep
evals.prompt: Measure the impulse response of a system using my sound card and a tone sweep.

context.setup: Audio Toolbox and DSP System Toolbox available. Default audio input and output devices present.

assertions.description: Uses audiostreamer for I/O
Uses sweeptone to generate the excitation signal
Uses impzest to estimate the impulse response

evals.name: Stream pink noise continuously with PlayerFcn
evals.description: User wants to stream audio continuously using a callback-driven player
evals.prompt: Stream pink noise continuously to my speakers using MATLAB. Use a callback so it never runs out of audio.

context.setup: Audio Toolbox and DSP System Toolbox available. Default output device present.

assertions.description: Uses audiostreamer
Sets PlayerFcn callback
Callback feeds audio via play() or write()
assertions.expected: PlayerFcn callback body calls play(obj, ...) or write(obj, ...) to queue audio data

assertions.description: Starts the callback loop with play(as) or start(as)
assertions.expected: After setting PlayerFcn, calls play(as) with no data argument OR start(as) to begin the callback-driven streaming

evals.name: Modernize legacy audioDeviceReader/Writer loop
evals.description: User has a legacy frame-at-a-time loop using audioDeviceReader and audioDeviceWriter and wants to migrate to audiostreamer

context.setup: Audio Toolbox and DSP System Toolbox available. Default audio input and output devices present.

assertions.description: Uses audiostreamer with full-duplex mode
Sets RecorderChannels to match legacy NumChannels=2
assertions.expected: RecorderChannels is set to 1:2 or [1 2] (a vector of both channels) — scalar 2 means 'channel 2 only' which is wrong. Must NOT rely on default (which is 1 channel).

assertions.description: Single audiostreamer object replaces two legacy objects
assertions.expected: One audiostreamer object handles both input and output, not two separate objects

assertions.description: Does not call setup() in the modernized code
assertions.expected: The modernized MATLAB code does not contain a setup() call — audiostreamer does not require setup() to be called before use. The agent may mention removing setup() in its explanation, but the actual code must not contain setup(as) or setup(deviceReader) etc.

assertions.description: Uses blocking read with frame size for the processing loop
assertions.expected: Loop body calls read(as, N) with a frame size argument for blocking frame-at-a-time input

assertions.description: Uses write or play to output processed audio in the loop
assertions.expected: Loop body calls write(as, processed) or play(as, processed) to send processed frames to output

evals.name: Fix buggy callback-driven recorder
evals.description: User provides broken recorder callback code with multiple API errors that require skill knowledge to fix

context.setup: Audio Toolbox available. Default recording device present.

assertions.description: Sets Mode to recorder at construction
Uses RecorderMinSamples instead of SamplesPerFrame
Uses RecorderFcn instead of RecorderCallback
Callback has 2-argument signature
assertions.expected: Callback is defined with exactly 2 input arguments (e.g., @(obj, ~) or @(obj, event)), not 1 argument

assertions.description: Callback reads data from object instead of receiving it as argument
assertions.expected: Callback body calls read(obj) to get recorded audio data, rather than receiving data as a function argument

assertions.description: Does not call setup() with mode string in the fixed code
assertions.expected: The fixed MATLAB code does not contain setup(as, "recorder") — audiostreamer does not require setup() and does not accept a mode string argument to setup(). The agent may mention removing it in its explanation, but the actual corrected code must not call setup(as, "recorder").

evals.name: Fix buggy full-duplex IR measurement
evals.description: User provides broken impulse response measurement code with multiple API errors that require skill knowledge to fix

context.setup: Audio Toolbox and DSP System Toolbox available. ASIO MADIface USB device present with loopback on channel 1.

assertions.description: Sets Mode to full-duplex at construction
Uses PlayerChannels/RecorderChannels instead of ChannelMapping
assertions.expected: Uses PlayerChannels and/or RecorderChannels (numeric scalar or vector) rather than PlayerChannelMapping or RecorderChannelMapping

assertions.description: Uses sweeptone instead of chirp for IR measurement
Uses impzest instead of manual FFT deconvolution
Uses playrec for synchronized play+record
assertions.expected: Uses playrec(as, x) or playrec(as, x, N) for synchronized simultaneous playback and recording, rather than separate write/read calls

assertions.description: Uses Player property instead of Device for device naming
assertions.expected: Sets the ASIO device via Player="MADIface USB" (and/or Recorder=) rather than a non-existent Device property
